item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The D-Class testing here definitely drags, but the final interview is effective and impactful enough to offset that issue.

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: The image used in this article is cc-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0). The background image comes from Wikimedia Commons, and the other component was author-created.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This isn't bad but it seems like rushed the containment procedures. Where is it held? Is it monitored? This should be addressed outside of the recovery log. Just saying otherwise neat treatment of this scary idea.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Very much enjoyed this. I was mostly pleased that it wasn't one of those "replaces personnel and the Foundation can't tell" skips. Like, it still sounds like they suspect something but can't say what because professionalism. But the people still stay themselves. It's like the DVD player, but one at a time.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That seems quite overreactive to me. For all you know, far more people liked the Samothrace bit and just happened to not have though of stating that in the comments.

I like it, BTW.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: bretty good, extremely underrated. and the image is excellent as well. Upvoted!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This combines the weird, creative feeling I get from series 2 skips with the narrative and complexity of series 4 stuff, leading this to be an excellent article in my opinion.

Very well done! +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The first section under Description feels a little off in tone. However, the rest of it is compelling, and I'm curious as to whether the Foundation's assessment that it's just a nonsentient mimic is actually true, a misunderstanding, or a complete fabrication. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: +1 for the feels

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is what I expect from series 4, a good and unique concept that is enriched with a story. I really like this, but I feel like adding one mroe test log would be good. Still though, +1 and really good job bud.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is one of those SCP's that prove we aren't losing creativeness on the wiki +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Great dialogue. Very effective SCP.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is very impressive for a first SCP. I always enjoy disquieting skips (pun intended). +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You're the second person to say the Samothrace bit was unnecessary, so I've cut it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
• They aren't - but they have to be very insistent about it, because the alternative is that they've been needlessly torturing genuine copies of valued employees.
  • There are like three million test logs that couldn't make it in because they were bloating the article. I tried to reference them in a few places but didn't get much farther than that. The Samothrace thing alludes to the fact that cognitohazards don't 'stick' from one replica to the next, which means there isn't a 'host consciousness' responsible for the replicas - at least not in the traditional sense of a consciousness. The comment about electrical testing alludes to the fact that they've built up a fairly comprehensive model of how currents/data pass through this thing, and that's what led them to the suspicion that old replicas aren't deleted when a new one is created. Ultimately, though, none of that made it into the article, which means they're relegated to my headcanons rather than actual canon.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's very SOMA, very Black Mirror. It's written well but there's just not enough here that sets it apart for me. The Samothrace reference felt a little gratuitous as well. I'm neutral on this one.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This reminds me a lot of the game SOMA, specifically the Vivarium and Movkbird concepts. A new nitpicks though:

  • How are they sure it’s non sentient?
  • Why haven’t they examined it in depth to see what exactly is it doing?

Otherwise, I liked this. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I wasn’t enjoying this too much until the last log tbh, but the convo between the researcher and their copy was extremely compelling to me. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This really resonates with me. The last log is sticking with me, and this just feels disturbing.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really like this but something feels, I don't know, off?

Still gonna give this a +1

item.title: With thanks to
item.content:encoded:
• sirpudding and BestManUSA for forum feedback and ideas
  • djkaktus for… everything, really
  • The Great Hippo for feedback, patience, and just generally being cool
  • Captain Kirby for guidance and tone
  • Everyone else who helped me work on my weird robot
  • #site19 for tolerating my plugs
  • And viewers like you!
